title: TypeScript, JavaScript, and the TypeScript 7 Toolchain · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: Trace a service-status module through TypeScript 7 checking, JavaScript emission, module loading, and runtime behavior. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a compiler-to-runtime evidence notebook with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish compile-time type evidence from emitted JavaScript and runtime validation.
Predict which TypeScript constructs erase, transform, or affect emitted JavaScript and source maps.
Use the TypeScript 7 native compiler and explain when a TypeScript 6 API compatibility tool remains necessary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab build checks a two-package TypeScript 7 workspace with strict mode, ESM output, and one TypeScript 6 API-dependent tool. Record the emitted JavaScript, diagnostic code, runtime result, and the compatibility boundary before release 6.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish compile-time type evidence from emitted javascript and runtime validation.
